model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Duke is the #1 overall seed with a 31-2 record and ranks #1 in both KenPom and Torvik ratings, featuring elite offense (rank 4) and defense (rank 1). While Siena earned their first tournament bid since 2010 by winning the MAAC, they are a significant underdog with a KenPom rank of 192 and no match for Duke's talent disparity. Despite Duke missing starting point guard Caleb Foster and big man Patrick Ngongba, the Blue Devils' depth and overall superiority make a 16-over-1 upset extremely unlikely.

reasoningStep.title: East Region 1 vs 16 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Duke is a historically dominant #1 seed facing a MAAC champion Siena team that is vastly outmatched in talent and metrics.
evidence: Duke is #1 in both KenPom (39.17 adjEM) and Torvik rankings with a 31-2 record, featuring top-4 offense and #1 defense.
Siena is ranked #192 in KenPom (-2.11 adjEM) with a 23-11 record from the MAAC conference.
Duke is missing starting PG Caleb Foster (foot fracture) and C Patrick Ngongba (foot), but still has superior depth and talent.
16 seeds have only beaten 1 seeds twice in NCAA Tournament history (UMBC over Virginia in 2018, FDU over Purdue in 2023), making such upsets extremely rare.
Siena's first tournament appearance since 2010 under coach Gerry McNamara, but they lack the athleticism and size to compete with Duke's roster.

result.snippet: What Is the Status of Caleb Foster ? result.text: Get To Know 2026 MAAC Tournament Champs: Siena Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know 2026 MAAC Tournament Champs: Siena Basketball By Mat Mlodzinski Mar 18, 2026 Siena basketball logo 20 years removed from his own March Madness legacy, Gerry McNamara is back in the NCAA Tournament, this time as the head coach of Siena basketball. Talk about a turnaround for the Siena basketball program. Two years ago, the Saints went 4-28 for the worst record in program history. But once G-Mac got to town, things changed immediately, with a 10-win improvement in year one, followed by a return to March Madness here in year two. Of course, the Saints are a 16 seed and have to play the top overall team, Duke, on Thursday. But McNamara is a fiery head coach who seems to get the most out of his guys. How they got there: After going 13-7 in the MAAC regular season, Siena got the 3-seed in the MAAC Tournament. And if you know anything about this conference tournament, it’s that it gets pretty wild. The last time the 1 seed won it was in 2023, when Rick Pitino took Iona to the big dance. More often than not, it’s someone you’d least expect cutting down the nets in Atlantic City, N.J. Of course, no shame in Siena doing that. The Saints put together a very good regular season and reached a new level defensively in the MAAC Tournament. They shut down Mount St. Mary’s, Fairfield, and Merrimack, each to below 65 points, playing their brand of basketball to the highest level possible. And it paid off. General information: Team: Siena Location: Loudonville, N.Y. Record: 23-11 (13-7) Head Coach: Gerry McNamara (2nd season overall, both at Siena) What to know about Siena basketball: The Saints have a defense-first identity, allowing just 65.7 points per game. And that’s partially due to not being able to rely on the three. They only hit 30.4% of shots from outside, so they don’t try to

result.text: Get To Know 2026 MAAC Tournament Champs: Siena Basketball Skip to content CBB Review (College Basketball Review) College Basketball Power Rankings, Interviews and more! ACC Boston College Cal Clemson Duke Florida State Georgia Tech Louisville Miami (Fla) NC State North Carolina Notre Dame Pitt SMU Stanford Syracuse Virginia Virginia Tech Wake Forest Big East Butler Creighton DePaul Georgetown Marquette Providence Seton Hall St. John’s UConn Villanova Xavier Big Ten Illinois Indiana Iowa Maryland Michigan Michigan State Minnesota Nebraska Northwestern Ohio State Oregon Penn State Purdue Rutgers UCLA USC Washington Wisconsin Big 12 Arizona Arizona State Baylor BYU Cincinnati Colorado Houston Iowa State Kansas Kansas State Oklahoma State TCU Texas Tech UCF Utah West Virginia SEC Alabama Arkansas Auburn Florida Georgia Kentucky LSU Mississippi State Missouri Oklahoma Ole Miss South Carolina Tennessee Texas Texas A&M Vanderbilt Mid Majors AAC Atlantic Sun Atlantic 10 C-USA Horizon MAAC MAC Missouri Valley Mountain West SoCon WAC WCC Oregon State Washington State Bracketology Matty Brackets March on CBB Review Rankings Meet Our Staff Get To Know 2026 MAAC Tournament Champs: Siena Basketball By Mat Mlodzinski Mar 18, 2026 Siena basketball logo 20 years removed from his own March Madness legacy, Gerry McNamara is back in the NCAA Tournament, this time as the head coach of Siena basketball. Talk about a turnaround for the Siena basketball program. Two years ago, the Saints went 4-28 for the worst record in program history. But once G-Mac got to town, things changed immediately, with a 10-win improvement in year one, followed by a return to March Madness here in year two. Of course, the Saints are a 16 seed and have to play the top overall team, Duke, on Thursday. But McNamara is a fiery head coach who seems to get the most out of his guys. How they got there: After going 13-7 in the MAAC regular season, Siena got the 3-seed in the MAAC Tournament. And if you know anything about this conference tournament, it’s that it gets pretty wild. The last time the 1 seed won it was in 2023, when Rick Pitino took Iona to the big dance. More often than not, it’s someone you’d least expect cutting down the nets in Atlantic City, N.J. Of course, no shame in Siena doing that. The Saints put together a very good regular season and reached a new level defensively in the MAAC Tournament. They shut down Mount St. Mary’s, Fairfield, and Merrimack, each to below 65 points, playing their brand of basketball to the highest level possible. And it paid off. General information: Team: Siena Location: Loudonville, N.Y. Record: 23-11 (13-7) Head Coach: Gerry McNamara (2nd season overall, both at Siena) What to know about Siena basketball: The Saints have a defense-first identity, allowing just 65.7 points per game.
